sentence: I need to ______ early tomorrow for school.
explanation: El phrasal verb <get up> significa levantarse.
tip: You do this every morning when you leave your bed.

explanation: El phrasal verb <sit down> significa sentarse.
tip: The opposite of standing.

sentence: Can you ______ the light? It's dark.
explanation: El phrasal verb <turn on> significa encender.
tip: You do this to a lamp when it's dark.

explanation: El phrasal verb <put on> significa ponerse ropa.
tip: You do this with clothes when you're cold.

explanation: El phrasal verb <take off> significa quitarse ropa o zapatos.
tip: The opposite of putting on.

sentence: Can you ______ that pen from the floor?
explanation: El phrasal verb <pick up> significa recoger algo.
tip: You do this when something is on the floor.

explanation: El phrasal verb <look at> significa mirar algo.
tip: You use your eyes to do this.

sentence: I'm ______ my keys. Have you seen them?
explanation: El phrasal verb <look for> significa buscar algo.
tip: You do this when you've lost something.

explanation: El phrasal verb <wake up> significa despertarse.
tip: The opposite of falling asleep.

sentence: We need to ______ home before dark.
explanation: El phrasal verb <go back> significa volver a un lugar.
tip: The opposite of going away from home.

explanation: El phrasal verb <turn off> significa apagar.
tip: The opposite of turning on.

explanation: El phrasal verb <stand up> significa ponerse de pie.
tip: The opposite of sitting down.

explanation: El phrasal verb <come in> significa entrar.
tip: You do this when you enter a room.

sentence: I want to ______ tonight with friends.
explanation: El phrasal verb <go out> significa salir.
tip: The opposite of staying home.

explanation: El phrasal verb <listen to> significa escuchar.
tip: You use your ears to do this.

sentence: Please ______ me. I'll be right back.
explanation: El phrasal verb <wait for> significa esperar.
tip: You do this when someone says 'I'll be right back'.

explanation: El phrasal verb <come back> significa regresar.
tip: You do this after a trip — you return.

explanation: El phrasal verb <give back> significa devolver.
tip: You do this when you return a borrowed item.

explanation: El phrasal verb <throw away> significa tirar a la basura.
tip: You do this with trash or old things.

explanation: El phrasal verb <write down> significa anotar.
tip: You do this with a pen and paper to remember something.

sentence: The children need to ______ before dinner.
explanation: El phrasal verb <clean up> significa limpiar.
tip: You do this to make a messy place tidy.

sentence: Can you ______ the blanks on this form?
explanation: El phrasal verb <fill in> significa rellenar.
tip: You do this with a form — add the missing information.

sentence: She wants to ______ with her friends tonight.
explanation: El phrasal verb <go out> significa salir.
tip: You do this on a Friday night with friends.

sentence: Please ______ your name on the paper.
explanation: El phrasal verb <write down> significa anotar.
tip: You do this with a pen to remember something.

sentence: Don't forget to ______ the lights when you leave.
explanation: El phrasal verb <turn off> significa apagar.
tip: You do this with lamps and TVs before leaving home.
